articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Lukaku double as Belgium outclass Luxembourg
article.description: Romelu Lukaku extended his Belgium scoring record as they ended their preparations for the European Championship with a 3-0 win over neighbours Luxembourg in Brussels on Saturday.
article.linkText: Lukaku nets brace as Belgium outclass Luxembourg

images.name: Belgium celeb vs. Luxembourg_20240608 [1296x729]
images.alt: Belgium's forward #10 Romelu Lukaku (R) celebrates scoring his team's first goal with Belgium's midfielder #24 Amadou Onana during the international friendly football match between Belgium and Luxembourg at the Baudoin King Stadium in Brussels on June 08,

article.story:
Romelu Lukaku extended his Belgium scoring record as they ended their preparations for the European Championship with a 3-0 win over neighbours Luxembourg in Brussels on Saturday.

Lukaku netted a 42nd-minute penalty for his 84th international goal and tapped in a second after being set up by Jeremy Doku's jinking run down the left and square pass.

Leandro Trossard added the third 10 minutes from time.

A near full-strength Belgium made a fast start but both Kevin De Bruyne and Lukaku were denied opportunities to open the scoring by a determined Luxembourg defence.

There was also a chance for Trossard while Doku troubled the visiting defence but tenacious tackling kept them at bay until Lukaku set off on a powerful run from the halfway line and won a penalty after a challenge by Enes Mahmutovic.

Lukaku scuffed the spot-kick but found the net and there was no stopping his effort at the back post when Doku set him up for his second after 57 minutes.

Trossard netted the third in the 80th minute, running from deep on to Wout Faes' long pass, bringing the ball under control and firing home.

It could have been more had Luxembourg's Belgian-born goalkeeper Anthony Moris not made several important saves after skipping his brother's wedding to play in the match.

There will be concern in the Belgian camp about the availability at Euro 2024 of full back Thomas Meunier, who had to leave the field after 18 minutes with what looked a thigh injury.

Belgium beat Montenegro 2-0 on Wednesday in their first warm-up game. Their players are getting a few days off before heading to Germany and their opening match at Euro 2024 against Slovenia in Frankfurt on June 17.

keyEvents.text: Substitution, Belgium. Maxim De Cuyper replaces Thomas Meunier because of an injury.
Goal!  Belgium 1, Luxembourg 0. Romelu Lukaku (Belgium) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, Belgium. Aster Vranckx replaces Axel Witsel.
Substitution, Luxembourg. Vahid Selimovic replaces Christopher Martins because of an injury.
Goal!  Belgium 2, Luxembourg 0. Romelu Lukaku (Belgium) left footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Jérémy Doku.
Substitution, Belgium. Yannick Carrasco replaces Jérémy Doku.
Substitution, Belgium. Johan Bakayoko replaces Timothy Castagne.
Substitution, Belgium. Zeno Debast replaces Amadou Onana.
Eldin Dzogovic (Luxembourg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Belgium. Charles De Ketelaere replaces Kevin De Bruyne.
Substitution, Luxembourg. Fabio Lohei replaces Laurent Jans.
Goal!  Belgium 3, Luxembourg 0. Leandro Trossard (Belgium) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Wout Faes with a through ball.
Substitution, Luxembourg. Jayson Videira replaces Gerson Rodrigues.
Substitution, Luxembourg. Sofiane Ikene replaces Chris Philipps.
Substitution, Luxembourg. David Jonathans replaces Eldin Dzogovic.
